question:
Find the minimum value of
\[\frac{(x+1/x)^6-(x^6+1/x^6)-2}{(x+1/x)^3+(x^3+1/x^3)}\]
for $x>0$.
solution:
Notice that
\begin{gather*}
\frac{(x+1/x)^6-(x^6+1/x^6)-2}{(x+1/x)^3+(x^3+1/x^3)} = \\
(x+1/x)^3-(x^3+1/x^3)=3(x+1/x)
\end{gather*}
(difference of squares).  The latter is easily seen
(e.g., by AM-GM) to have minimum value 6
(achieved at $x=1$).

descriptive_long.question:
Find the minimum value of
\[\frac{(\positivex+1/\positivex)^6-(\positivex^6+1/\positivex^6)-2}{(\positivex+1/\positivex)^3+(\positivex^3+1/\positivex^3)}\]
for $\positivex>0$.
descriptive_long.solution:
Notice that
\begin{gather*}
\frac{(\positivex+1/\positivex)^6-(\positivex^6+1/\positivex^6)-2}{(\positivex+1/\positivex)^3+(\positivex^3+1/\positivex^3)} = \\
(\positivex+1/\positivex)^3-(\positivex^3+1/\positivex^3)=3(\positivex+1/\positivex)
\end{gather*}
(difference of squares).  The latter is easily seen
(e.g., by AM-GM) to have minimum value 6
(achieved at $\positivex=1$).

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
Find the minimum value of
\[\frac{(candlewax+1/candlewax)^6-(candlewax^6+1/candlewax^6)-2}{(candlewax+1/candlewax)^3+(candlewax^3+1/candlewax^3)}\]
for $candlewax>0$.
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
Notice that
\begin{gather*}
\frac{(candlewax+1/candlewax)^6-(candlewax^6+1/candlewax^6)-2}{(candlewax+1/candlewax)^3+(candlewax^3+1/candlewax^3)} = \\
(candlewax+1/candlewax)^3-(candlewax^3+1/candlewax^3)=3(candlewax+1/candlewax)
\end{gather*}
(difference of squares).  The latter is easily seen
(e.g., by AM-GM) to have minimum value 6
(achieved at $candlewax=1$).

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Find the minimum value of
\[\frac{(constantvalue+1/constantvalue)^6-(constantvalue^6+1/constantvalue^6)-2}{(constantvalue+1/constantvalue)^3+(constantvalue^3+1/constantvalue^3)}\]
for $constantvalue>0$.
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
Notice that
\begin{gather*}
\frac{(constantvalue+1/constantvalue)^6-(constantvalue^6+1/constantvalue^6)-2}{(constantvalue+1/constantvalue)^3+(constantvalue^3+1/constantvalue^3)} = \\
(constantvalue+1/constantvalue)^3-(constantvalue^3+1/constantvalue^3)=3(constantvalue+1/constantvalue)
\end{gather*}
(difference of squares).  The latter is easily seen
(e.g., by AM-GM) to have minimum value 6
(achieved at $constantvalue=1$).

garbled_string.question:
Find the minimum value of
\[\frac{(qzxwvtnp+1/qzxwvtnp)^6-(qzxwvtnp^6+1/qzxwvtnp^6)-2}{(qzxwvtnp+1/qzxwvtnp)^3+(qzxwvtnp^3+1/qzxwvtnp^3)}\]
for $qzxwvtnp>0$.
garbled_string.solution:
Notice that
\begin{gather*}
\frac{(qzxwvtnp+1/qzxwvtnp)^6-(qzxwvtnp^6+1/qzxwvtnp^6)-2}{(qzxwvtnp+1/qzxwvtnp)^3+(qzxwvtnp^3+1/qzxwvtnp^3)} = \\
(qzxwvtnp+1/qzxwvtnp)^3-(qzxwvtnp^3+1/qzxwvtnp^3)=3(qzxwvtnp+1/qzxwvtnp)
\end{gather*}
(difference of squares).  The latter is easily seen
(e.g., by AM-GM) to have minimum value 6
(achieved at $qzxwvtnp=1$).

kernel_variant.question:
For every real \(t>0\) find the minimum of  
\[
\frac{(t+1/t)^{8}\;-\bigl(t^{4}+1/t^{4}\bigr)^{2}}
     {(t+1/t)^{4}+\bigl(t^{4}+1/t^{4}\bigr)}.
\]

_replacement_note.reason: Original kernel variant was too easy compared to the original problem
